title: Hard-window AUC specialist with pairwise ranking loss
summary: ROC AUC is the competition metric, and AUC-optimization literature shows error-rate or log-loss training is not always best for ranking.
rationale: ROC AUC is the competition metric, and AUC-optimization literature shows error-rate or log-loss training is not always best for ranking. In this task, the hardest distinctions are late-stint laps that look similar but differ in immediate pit probability, so a small specialist focused on these pairs may add rank signal even if global calibration is unchanged.
implementation_hint: Train a second-stage model only on ambiguous windows, e.g. positives plus hard negatives from similar `RaceProgress`, `Compound`, and `TyreLife` ranges, using pairwise/ranking or explicit AUC-surrogate loss. Blend it only on rows where the base model is uncertain or late-stint.
expected_effect: Improves ordering among near-decision laps, where public-LB movement is often driven by subtle ranking errors rather than average probability quality.
risk: May help local AUC but hurt global robustness if the ambiguity slice is defined too narrowly or with leakage-prone labels.
